metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda (no Special of 28/10/2022)
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Parliament; promulgated by the President)
metadata.source_note: Official Gazette no Special of 28/10/2022 print of Law No 027/2022 of 20/10/2022 establishing taxes on income, served by the Rwanda Revenue Authority document library (text-embedded trilingual print; the English column is citable). Grounds: Article 56 withholding tax on employment income with Table 1 ("First year following the date of commencement of this law": 0-60,000 FRW at 0 percent, 60,001-100,000 at 20 percent, 100,001 and more at 30 percent) and Table 2 ("From the second year after the commencement of this law": 0-60,000 at 0 percent, 60,001-100,000 at 10 percent, 100,001-200,000 at 20 percent, 200,001 and more at 30 percent) plus the casual-labourer special rate of fifteen percent with income not exceeding 60,000 FRW per month rated at zero percent; Article 8 tax period (calendar year, 1 January to 31 December); the micro-enterprise flat lump-sum table (annual turnover 2,000,000-4,000,000 FRW pays 60,000; 4,000,001-7,000,000 pays 120,000; 7,000,001-10,000,000 pays 210,000; 10,000,001-12,000,000 pays 300,000) and the small-enterprise lump-sum of three percent of annual turnover, with the definitions fixing small business turnover between 12,000,001 and 20,000,000 FRW and micro-enterprise at or below 12,000,000; Article 21 exemption for agricultural or livestock turnover not exceeding 12,000,000 FRW in a tax period (with the excess-only inclusion rule); and Article 73 commencement on publication in the Official Gazette. The 2023 amendment (Law No 051/2023) touches declaration timing and the corporate rate only, and the 27 May 2025 tax package left these brackets unamended, so the Table 2 schedule stands for CY2025.
metadata.title: Law No 027/2022 of 20/10/2022 establishing taxes on income - Article 56 PAYE monthly schedules and casual-labour rate; lump-sum regime; Article 21 agricultural exemption; Article 8 tax period; Article 73 commencement

metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda (no Special of 14/09/2023), gazettes.africa archive facsimile
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Parliament; promulgated by the President)
metadata.source_note: Full Official Gazette no Special of 14/09/2023 (147-page text-embedded trilingual print carrying Laws No 048/2023, 049/2023, 050/2023 and 051/2023), served by the gazettes.africa archive facsimile mirror; the Rwanda Revenue Authority library hosts standalone prints of the VAT and income-tax-amendment laws from the same issue but not of Law No 048/2023, so the gazette issue is captured whole with the host recorded. Grounds Law No 048/2023 determining the sources of revenue and property of decentralised entities (which repeals Law no 75/2018 of 07/09/2018): Article 42 rental income tax rates (0 percent for annual rental income from one FRW to 180,000; 20 percent from 180,001 to 1,000,000; 30 percent above 1,000,000) and Article 43 taxable rental income (gross rental income minus 50 percent considered as maintenance and upkeep expenses). RWAMOD RW_2025 applies exactly this base and band structure to individual rental income.
metadata.title: Law No 048/2023 of 05/09/2023 determining the sources of revenue and property of decentralised entities - Articles 42-43 rental income tax rates and 50 percent deemed-expense base

metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda (no Special of 14/09/2023), Rwanda Revenue Authority standalone print
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Parliament; promulgated by the President)
metadata.source_note: Rwanda Revenue Authority standalone print of Law No 049/2023 of 05/09/2023 on value added tax from the Official Gazette no Special of 14/09/2023 (55-page text-embedded trilingual print). Grounds Article 4: the rate of value added tax is 0 percent of the value of taxable goods or services that are zero-rated as provided for by the Law, or 18 percent for other goods and services supplied in Rwanda or imported. The May 2025 amendment (Law No 009/2025) does not change the standard rate, so 18 percent stands for CY2025. RWAMOD RW_2025 applies the 18 percent standard rate on the household consumption base.
metadata.title: Law No 049/2023 of 05/09/2023 on value added tax - Article 4 rate of value added tax (18 percent standard)

metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda (no Special of 29/05/2025), Rwanda Revenue Authority standalone print
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Parliament; promulgated by the President)
metadata.source_note: Rwanda Revenue Authority standalone print of Law No 011/2025 of 27/05/2025 on excise duty (46-page text-embedded trilingual print). Grounds the excise schedule rows the RW_2025 model year applies: cigarettes at 36 percent of retail price tax inclusive of a pack of 20 rods plus FRW 230 per pack; wine made from at least 70 percent local raw material at 40 percent and other wines at 70 percent (of the value of a litre not exceeding FRW 40,000); brandies/liquors/whisky with the local-content split (60 percent local, 70 percent other); beer with at least 70 percent local raw material at 40 percent and other beers at 65 percent; soft drinks/sodas; juices; mineral and other bottled water; premium fuel (excluding benzene) at FRW 183 per litre and gas oil at FRW 150 per litre; lubricants; motor vehicles by engine-capacity class; airtime/telephone and internet-telecom services at 12 percent; chocolates and chewing gum per kilogramme; electronic cigarettes per unit and e-liquid cartridges; cigars at 160 percent; and beauty/make-up cosmetics. Article 18 repeals Law No 050/2023 of 05/09/2023 (whose corresponding rows carried 36 percent plus FRW 130 per pack for cigarettes and 30 percent for local wine) and Article 19 brings the law into force on publication (Official Gazette no Special of 29/05/2025); the model's values match the 2025 schedule, so effective dating follows this print.
metadata.title: Law No 011/2025 of 27/05/2025 on excise duty - schedule of ad-valorem and specific rates (cigarettes 36 percent plus FRW 230 per pack; wines, beers, brandies; soft drinks; fuels FRW 183/150 per litre; e-cigarettes; cosmetics)

metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda; Rwanda Legal Information Institute (Laws.Africa) AKN source facsimile
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Parliament; promulgated by the President)
metadata.source_note: Rwanda Legal Information Institute (rwandalii.org, the Laws.Africa AKN mirror of the Official Gazette text) source PDF of Law No 05/2015 of 30/03/2015 governing the organisation of pension schemes (84-page text-embedded print) - mirror host recorded; the official portal does not serve a standalone print. Grounds the mandatory-scheme contribution structure: the employer and the employee contribute equally to the pension scheme (with differential rates possible by nature of employment), and the contribution rate is determined by a Presidential Order. Presidential Order No 086/01 of 12/12/2024 (captured separately in the regulation-class manifest) fixes the rate ladder from 1 January 2025 at 12 percent, which under the equal-sharing rule RWAMOD RW_2025 applies as 6 percent employee plus 6 percent employer.
metadata.title: Law No 05/2015 of 30/03/2015 governing the organisation of pension schemes - equal employer/employee sharing of the mandatory pension contribution; delegation of the rate to a Presidential Order

metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda (no Special of 20/04/2016); Ministry of Public Service and Labour document library
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Parliament; promulgated by the President)
metadata.source_note: Ministry of Public Service and Labour (MIFOTRA) library print of Law No 003/2016 of 30/03/2016 establishing and governing the maternity leave benefits scheme (19-page text-embedded trilingual print; the MIFOTRA TYPO3 dumpFile URL carries a token that may rotate - re-fetch via the MIFOTRA publications page if it does). Grounds Article 7: the contribution for maternity leave benefits is equal to zero point six percent (0.6%) of the salary to which the contribution is subscribed, with the employer and the employee each contributing zero point three percent (0.3%). Law No 049/2024 (amendment) extends leave duration and benefit calculation but does not change the contribution rate, so 0.3 + 0.3 stands for CY2025. RWAMOD RW_2025 applies exactly this split on gross employment income; the contribution base components are fixed by Ministerial Order No 007/16/10/TC of 28/10/2016 (captured separately in the regulation-class manifest).
metadata.title: Law No 003/2016 of 30/03/2016 establishing and governing the maternity leave benefits scheme - Article 7 contribution rate (0.6 percent of salary; employer and employee each 0.3 percent)

metadata.publisher: Rwanda Social Security Board document library (English consolidated print)
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Parliament; promulgated by the President)
metadata.source_note: Rwanda Social Security Board library English print of Law No 24/2001 of 27/04/2001 establishing RAMA - La Rwandaise d'Assurance Maladie, the medical insurance scheme for public servants, consolidated with the Law No 29/2002 modifications (12-page text-embedded print; the administering institution's own consolidated reproduction, host recorded). Grounds Article 44: the contribution rate due to RAMA is 15 percent of the employee's basic salary, shared out with the employer paying 7.5 percent and the employee paying the remaining 7.5 percent (plus the 15 percent moderating fee on services, not a payroll contribution). RAMA is administered by RSSB since the 2010 merger; RWAMOD RW_2025 applies 7.5 + 7.5 percent to formal public-sector employees and 7.5 percent to pensioners on their pension income.
metadata.title: Law No 24/2001 of 27/04/2001 establishing RAMA (consolidated with Law No 29/2002) - Article 44 contribution (15 percent of basic salary shared 7.5 percent employer / 7.5 percent employee)

metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda; Rwanda Legal Information Institute (Laws.Africa) AKN source facsimile
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Parliament; promulgated by the President)
metadata.source_note: Rwanda Legal Information Institute (rwandalii.org, Laws.Africa AKN mirror) source PDF of Law No 03/2015 of 02/03/2015 governing the organisation of the community-based health insurance scheme (12-page text-embedded print; mirror host recorded). Grounds the scheme framework and the delegation under which CBHI contribution amounts and payment modalities are determined by Orders - the operative instruments are the Prime Minister's Order No 105/2020 of 30/09/2020 (employee 0.5 percent of net salary) and the Ministerial Order No 002/26/10/TC of 16/02/2026 (member premiums by IMIBEREHO Dynamic Social Registry level), both captured in the regulation-class manifest.
metadata.title: Law No 03/2015 of 02/03/2015 governing the organisation of the community-based health insurance scheme - delegation of contribution amounts and payment modalities to Orders

metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda (no Special of 15/12/2022), Military Medical Insurance document library
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Parliament; promulgated by the President)
metadata.source_note: Military Medical Insurance library print of the Official Gazette no Special of 15/12/2022 carrying Law No 036/2022 of 09/12/2022 establishing Military Medical Insurance (33-page text-embedded trilingual print). The current establishing law defers the determination of contribution rates for the medical care scheme to a Prime Minister's Order, which is not publicly digitized; the rate split RWAMOD applies (insured 5 percent, government 17.5 percent) is carried verbatim by the predecessor Law No 23/2005 Article 30 (captured alongside) and confirmed as current by the RWAMOD country report. Captured for the delegation chain.
metadata.title: Law No 036/2022 of 09/12/2022 establishing Military Medical Insurance - scheme framework; contribution rates deferred to a Prime Minister's Order

metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda (no Special of 15/12/2022), Military Medical Insurance document library

metadata.source_note: Military Medical Insurance library print of the Official Gazette no Special of 15/12/2022 carrying Law No 036/2022 of 09/12/2022 establishing Military Medical Insurance (33-page text-embedded trilingual print). The current establishing law defers the determination of contribution rates for the medical care scheme to a Prime Minister's Order, which is not publicly digitized; the rate split RWAMOD applies (insured 5 percent, government 17.5 percent) is carried verbatim by the predecessor Law No 23/2005 Article 30 (captured alongside) and confirmed as current by the RWAMOD country report. metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda; Rwanda Legal Information Institute (Laws.Africa) AKN source facsimile
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Parliament; promulgated by the President)
metadata.source_note: Rwanda Legal Information Institute (rwandalii.org, Laws.Africa AKN mirror) source PDF of Law No 23/2005 of 12/12/2005 establishing Military Medical Insurance and determining its organisation and functioning (30-page text-embedded print; mirror host recorded). Grounds Article 30: every month's subscription is twenty two and a half per cent (22.5%) of the basic salary of an insured person; regarding a soldier, the government contributes seventeen and a half per cent (17.5%) while the insured pays five per cent (5%). Law No 036/2022 replaces this law and defers rates to a Prime Minister's Order not publicly digitized; this print is the gazetted primary carrier of the 5/17.5 split that RWAMOD RW_2025 applies to the military/police employment class.
metadata.title: Law No 23/2005 of 12/12/2005 establishing Military Medical Insurance - Article 30 subscription (22.5 percent of basic salary; government 17.5 percent, insured 5 percent)

metadata.publisher: Rwanda Social Security Board document library (English print)
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Transitional National Assembly; promulgated by the President)
metadata.source_note: Rwanda Social Security Board library English print of Law No 06/2003 of 22/03/2003 modifying and completing the Decree-Law of 22 August 1974 concerning organization of social security (18-page text-embedded print; the administering institution's own reproduction, host recorded). Grounds Article 10 (modifying Article 14 of the Decree-Law): contributions for the branch of pensions are shared out equally between the employee and the employer, and contributions for the branch of occupational hazards are exclusively paid by the employer; and Article 9 (modifying Article 13): the percentage rates of contributions are fixed by Presidential Decree. The occupational-hazards rate-fixing decree itself is not publicly digitized; the 2 percent employer rate RWAMOD RW_2025 applies is documented only by the RSSB scheme administration (the model's own citation), so the rate is handled as a documented parity disposition rather than an encoded value.
metadata.title: Law No 06/2003 of 22/03/2003 modifying the Decree-Law of 22 August 1974 organizing social security - employer-exclusive occupational hazards contributions; rates fixed by Presidential Decree
